techniques.evidence_quote: a step in the GitHub Actions workflow inadvertently wrote the GitHub Actions Runner's environment variables, including sensitive AWS access keys, to a JavaScript file... served publicly as part of TinaCloud, allowing attackers to obtain the access keys directly from the front-end code
AWS access keys with root permissions were compromised and misused
These keys were exploited to send unauthorized emails... using our Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) infrastructure
send unauthorized emails (targeting the general French community, not Tina customers specifically)

attack_chain.description: A misconfigured GitHub Actions workflow wrote the runner's environment variables (including AWS access keys) into a JavaScript file that was deployed and served publicly as part of TinaCloud's front-end code.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a step in the GitHub Actions workflow inadvertently wrote the GitHub Actions Runner's environment variables, including sensitive AWS access keys, to a JavaScript file

attack_chain.description: Attackers harvested the exposed AWS access keys from the publicly served JavaScript file on the TinaCloud front-end.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: allowing attackers to obtain the access keys directly from the front-end code

evidence_chunks.quote: allowing attackers to obtain the access keys directly from the front-end code
allowing attackers to obtain the access keys directly from the front-end code

attack_chain.description: Attackers authenticated to AWS using the compromised access keys, which carried root-level permissions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: AWS access keys with root permissions were compromised and misused

evidence_chunks.quote: AWS access keys with root permissions were compromised and misused
AWS access keys with root permissions were compromised and misused

attack_chain.description: Attackers abused the hijacked Amazon SES infrastructure to send unauthorized phishing emails targeting the general French community.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: These keys were exploited to send unauthorized emails (targeting the general French community, not Tina customers specifically) using our Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) infrastructure

evidence_chunks.quote: These keys were exploited to send unauthorized emails (targeting the general French community, not Tina customers specifically) using our Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) infrastructure
These keys were exploited to send unauthorized emails (targeting the general French community, not Tina customers specifically) using our Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) infrastructure

attack_chain.description: The unauthorized emails sent through the victim's SES were phishing messages (in French) sent to external recipients.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A screenshot of one of the phishing emails

evidence_chunks.quote: A screenshot of one of the phishing emails
A screenshot of one of the phishing emails

initial_access_vector: AWS access keys with root permissions were written into a JavaScript file by a GitHub Actions build step and deployed publicly, where attackers harvested them from the TinaCloud front-end code.
persistence_mechanism: None established; attackers relied on the long-lived compromised AWS access keys until they were revoked.
privilege_escalation: Not required; the leaked AWS access keys already carried root-level permissions.
